results.title: Achieving Zero Road Fatalities Through Urban Design Adaptation and International Best Practices
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority continuously reviews road design to enhance safety, exemplified by the 2023 Friendly Streets initiative. This programme lowers speed limits and deploys traffic-calming infrastructure like 3D road markings and speed humps to improve walking and cycling safety. While international "Vision Zero" approaches are acknowledged, specific adaptation timelines or projected fatality reduction targets were not stated in the response. The focus remains on ongoing infrastructure enhancements rather than a fixed deadline for achieving zero fatalities.

results.title: Accident Risk for Taxi and Private Hire Car Drivers As a Result of Distance Travelled and Driver Fatigue
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport does not assess accident risk solely based on exposure metrics like distance travelled but evaluates risk holistically. Taxi and private hire car drivers receive training on occupational health hazards, including fatigue awareness and management, as part of their Vocational Licence course. Additionally, platform companies are required to ensure their drivers and delivery riders using active mobility devices on public paths maintain adequate third-party liability insurance coverage during work. The Ministry and Ministry of Manpower collaborate with platform providers to enhance workplace safety for these workers.

results.title: Addressing Migrant Worker Passport Retention Violations and Ensuring Compliance with Passports Act
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) enforces the Passports Act by ensuring migrant workers retain unrestricted access to their passports at all times. MOM raises awareness through the Settling-In Programme, the FWMOMCare app, social media, and email circulars, instructing workers to report violations. Employers who fail to return passports promptly face enforcement actions, including potential debarment from hiring new foreign employees. While over 31% of Work Permit holders did not keep their passports in the 2024 survey, MOM notes that most employers return passports after intervention.

results.title: Approval Process and Project Realisation Timelines for Low-carbon Electricity Imports Approved by Energy Market Authority
results.summary: The Energy Market Authority (EMA) assesses delivery likelihood for low-carbon electricity imports based on developers securing regulatory approvals, power purchase agreements, financing, and meeting technical milestones like subsea surveys. Projects meeting these criteria within one to two years could commence commercial operations by the end of the decade. EMA will continue engaging companies with credible proposals while the Government pursues other decarbonisation pathways, including maximising domestic solar, assessing hydrogen and nuclear potential, sourcing carbon credits, and investing in a second Liquefied Natural Gas terminal ready by 2030. No specific contingency measures for project delays or non-realisation were detailed in the response.

results.title: Breakdown of Cases Reported under Tripartite Framework for the Prevention of Abuse and Harassment in Healthcare by Severity and Sector
results.summary: Since the 2023 launch of the Tripartite Framework for the Prevention of Abuse and Harassment in Healthcare, Public Healthcare Institutions reported 3,000 incidents in 2024 and 4,200 in 2025. Approximately 7% of these cases were classified as high severity, while about 15% were reported to the Police. The Minister noted that reporting rates have increased due to heightened awareness following the framework's implementation. Specific breakdowns by sector and detailed trend analysis beyond these aggregate figures were not provided in the response.

results.title: Breakdown of Early Termination of Work Permit Holders by Sector and Reason for Termination and Tracking Premature Attrition Patterns in Last Five Years
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, the Ministry of Manpower published data on Work Permit cancellations within the first six months, excluding migrant domestic workers. The primary reason for cancellation is the termination of employment, though specific reasons like unsuitability or personal matters are not systematically collected. A sector-by-sector breakdown of these early terminations is provided in the accompanying table. The Ministry does not track patterns indicative of premature attrition beyond these cancellation statistics.

results.title: Breakdown of SME Employment Size Distribution Across by Employment Band in 2025
results.summary: As of 2024, Singapore had 356,600 SMEs with fewer than 200 workers, where 94.7% employed fewer than 25 workers, 3.1% employed 25 to 49 workers, 1.5% employed 50 to 99 workers, 0.5% employed 100 to 149 workers, and 0.2% employed 150 to 199 workers. The Minister for Manpower stated that data for 2025 is not yet available. Consequently, the breakdown of SME employment size distribution across these specific bands for 2025 remains untracked and will be updated once the relevant statistics are released.

results.title: Breakthroughs and Successes Attributed to A*STAR and Considerations to Reform Operational Model and Policies
results.summary: A*STAR is ranked 21st globally in the Nature Index, with 28% of its publications in the top 10% cited. Recent breakthroughs include semiconductor modulators operating at 400 billion conversions per second and the Fortitude test kit supplied to over 40 countries. Over five years, A*STAR spinoffs created more than 70 companies and 900 jobs, with entities like Mirxes operating in eight countries. Collaborations with Rolls-Royce yielded 18 technologies, while the T-Up Programme helped participating SMEs achieve 44% higher revenue and 22% higher employment within three years. Under Research, Innovation and Enterprise 2030, A*STAR will focus on sectors with strong capabilities and clear industry demand to strengthen pull-through and ensure research investments translate into tangible economic outcomes.

results.title: Cancer Drug Claims That Required Additional Documentation Beyond National Claims Processing System
results.summary: The Ministry of Health states that additional documentation is required for a minority of cancer claims, primarily involving non-Cancer Drug List (non-CDL) drugs where insurers need case-specific information to verify regulatory approval or clinical guideline support. This documentation is requested individually to ensure accurate assessment and correct benefit payment. The Ministry does not track the specific number of such claims nor their average processing duration outside the national claims processing system. No review of measures to streamline these documentation requirements was mentioned in the response.

results.title: Clarification by Minister of State for Manpower
results.summary: The Minister of State for Manpower clarified that the Workfare Income Supplement qualifying monthly wage cap remains at $3,000, targeting Singaporean workers in the bottom 20% of earners while providing limited support to those slightly above this threshold. Concurrently, annual Workfare Income Supplement payments will be increased to a maximum of $4,900. These factual corrections address previous statements made during the 3 March 2026 sitting, confirming the continued focus on low-income earners without altering the specific wage eligibility criteria.

results.title: Commercial Landlords Imposing Utilities Surcharges on Tenants beyond Base Rent and Gross Turnover Rent
results.summary: The Government lacks data on the prevalence of utility surcharges beyond base rent and Gross Turnover rent in the Food and Beverage sector. While the Code of Conduct for Leasing of Retail Premises mandates that commercial landlords pass through electricity costs without mark-up, no feedback has been received regarding surcharges on water or gas. The Fair Tenancy Industry Committee (FTIC) administers this code and invites businesses or individuals to submit details of such cases via their online enquiry form for follow-up. The Ministry has not indicated plans to mandate cost-only pass-throughs for all utilities.

results.title: Committee of Supply Reporting Progress
results.summary: The Committee of Supply reported progress on the Estimates of Expenditure for the financial year 2026/2027. Ms Grace Fu Hai Yen moved to report this progress and requested leave to sit again the following day. No specific expenditure figures, programme details, or policy measures were disclosed in this brief procedural update. The committee will resume its work on the 2026/2027 budget estimates at the next sitting.

results.title: Completion Timeline of Bus Fleet Management System Upgrade and Penalties Enforced for Agreement Breaches
results.summary: The Expected Time of Arrival system was fully restored on 12 February 2026 after physical rectifications and verification of bus arrival records. Financial penalties will be imposed on the third-party vendor for service-level agreement breaches. The Bus Fleet Management System upgrade, which began in 2024, is projected to be progressively completed by 2027. The Land Transport Authority provided regular progress updates until all buses were rectified.

results.title: Consolidation of Small Medical Practices by Corporate Chains and Supporting Viability of Independent Clinics
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not monitor the consolidation of independent clinics into corporate chains but regulates clinical standards and ethical practices. To support small practices complying with the Health Information Act (HIA), the Government will provide funding for procuring compliant IT systems and training. These measures aim to assist healthcare providers in meeting regulatory requirements without specifying turnkey IT solutions or dedicated implementation assistance. The response addresses funding and training support while omitting details on turnkey solutions or implementation assistance.

results.title: Criteria for Implementation Timeline of Distance-based Charging under ERP 2.0
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport will not implement distance-based charging under ERP 2.0 in the immediate term to allow motorists time to adapt to the new system. While usage-based charging is considered fair in principle, the Ministry will study impacts on specific groups, such as those driving for work who may face higher charges. If distance-based charging is pursued in the future, the Ministry will provide adequate public notice before implementation. No specific timeline or periodic update schedule for these parameters was confirmed.

results.title: Data on Appeals Related to Ethnic Integration Policy, Appeals Approved and Appeals That Led to Units Being Acquired by HDB
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that questions regarding 2025 HDB Ethnic Integration Policy appeal statistics and unit ownership as of 31 December 2025 were not answered in the current session. Instead, the Minister directed Members to refer to the Ministry of National Development's previous reply provided to Ms Valerie Lee on 25 February 2026. No new data, timelines, or operational constraints were introduced during this debate.

results.title: Data on Mediation, Warning and Police Escalation for Internally Reported Cases of Harassment of Healthcare Workers Under Tripartite Framework
results.summary: Under the Tripartite Framework for the Prevention of Abuse and Harassment in Healthcare, public healthcare institutions handle reported incidents on the spot or within the same day. Actions include activating supervisors, warning perpetrators, and issuing written warnings or barring abusive visitors. Serious cases involving physical harm are reported to the Police, where investigation duration depends on case complexity. The Minister noted that follow-up actions are adopted as appropriate after initial de-escalation. Specific statistics on median response times or case outcome distributions were not provided in the response.

results.title: Data on Missing Persons Cases Resolved Through Public Information, Social Media Appeals and Digital Tracking in 2024 and 2025
results.summary: The Police do not track the average time taken to locate a missing person, noting that response times vary significantly by case. While investigations prioritize vulnerable individuals, typically finding the majority within a day, the Police pursue all available leads including working with online service providers and publishing appeals. Consequently, specific statistics regarding the proportion of cases resolved through public information or digital tracking in 2024 and 2025 are not tracked or disclosed. The agency maintains that investigations are dynamic and specific to each case, preventing the aggregation of these metrics.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head L (Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment)
results.summary: Singapore designates 2026 as the Year of Climate Adaptation, with the National Adaptation Plan scheduled for publication in 2027 to address heat, flood, and water resilience. The Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment clarified that while "diversifying imports" spreads sourcing risk, "global partnerships" involve deeper collaboration, noting that recycling rates fell to their lowest point in 2024 with household plastic recycling at just 5%. To improve waste management, a 10-cent deposit default applies to non-participating food outlets, and Return Right Reverse Vending Machines will be deployed near most HDB coffeeshops within a five-minute walk. The government emphasizes that regulations alone are insufficient for issues like high-rise littering and second-hand smoke, requiring collective action from businesses and communities to support the transition to a circular economy and climate-ready infrastructure.

results.title: Data on Submission and Approval Rates of Maintenance Schedule Waiver Requests Submitted by Operators
results.summary: Since 30 May 2025, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has received and approved one maintenance schedule waiver request from rail operators. This specific approval granted a one-month deferment for air-conditioning filter replacement on four Thomson-East Coast Line trains used for testing works. LTA assesses such requests based on safety and service reliability impacts, the adequacy of operator mitigating measures, and longer-term effects on asset performance. No other waiver requests have been submitted or rejected as of the reporting date.

results.title: Differences in Mandate and Work Scope between Small- and Medium-sized Enterprise Pro-Enterprise Office and Pro-Enterprise Panel
results.summary: The SME Pro-Enterprise Office (SME PEO), established in March 2025 under Enterprise Singapore, functions as a central coordination unit to strengthen regulatory responsiveness for small- and medium-sized enterprises, complementing the existing Pro-Enterprise Panel (PEP). While the PEP, formed in 2000 and chaired by the Head of Civil Service, provides a public-private platform for reviewing rules across sectors, the SME PEO specifically addresses cross-agency regulatory issues and emerging sector challenges. Since its launch, the SME PEO has engaged over 170 business leaders across 15 sectors, reviewed more than 300 feedback items, and successfully resolved over 65% of raised issues. Notable outcomes include the streamlining of approval processes by the Urban Redevelopment Authority and Singapore Land Authority, which halved the time required for developers to obtain approvals for private residential show-flat sites.

results.title: Early SkillsFuture Support Top-up for Retrenched Individuals under Age 40
results.summary: The $4,000 Mid-Career SkillsFuture Credit top-up is restricted to Singaporeans aged 40 and above. Retrenched individuals under 40 are directed to alternative career transition programmes, including Workforce Singapore's Career Conversion Programmes and IMDA's TechSkills Accelerator, which offer substantial government funding for eligible participants. These initiatives facilitate job placement with salary support during company-led training or provide up to 12 months of training via SkillsFuture Career Transition Programmes. The Government will continue monitoring training needs and calibrate support accordingly.

results.title: Effort and Resources Expended for 2016 Separation of Workforce Development Agency into Two Agencies, and Current Re-merger Plan
results.summary: The 2016 separation of the Workforce Development Agency into SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore addressed structural barriers between the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Manpower to sharpen focus on continuing education and employment facilitation respectively. The current re-merger creates a single agency jointly overseen by both ministries to tighten integration between skills and jobs, leveraging labour market intelligence and education system oversight for holistic support. This structural change is not a reversal but an adaptation to a rapidly ageing workforce and accelerating technological advancements, aiming to pivot nimbly to future work challenges. The merger is not driven by cost savings, as the 2016 restructuring did not require additional funding, nor is the current merger expected to incur additional funding. Instead, it seeks to rationalize common functions over time and ensure agencies remain fit for purpose as the labour market evolves.

results.title: Efforts to Alleviate Traffic Congestion During Peak Hours along Loyang Avenue and Adjacent Roads
results.summary: To mitigate traffic congestion during peak hours along Loyang Avenue caused by the Loyang Viaduct and Cross Island Line Loyang station construction, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has enhanced alternative routes including Pasir Ris Drive 3 and Loyang Way. Service improvements include two new peak period Loyang-Changi Shuttle services and increased bus frequency where feasible. The project, scheduled for completion in 2030, aims to improve travel times for residents. While additional road closures may occur during specific work phases, these will be prioritized for off-peak periods unless safety-critical. During continuous daytime works, LTA deploys extra road signs, ground staff, and adjusts traffic light timings to manage flow. The Authority will monitor conditions and adjust measures as necessary.

results.title: Efforts to Increase Availability and Uptake of Screening for Common Types of Cancer
results.summary: Under Healthier SG, regular screening for breast, cervical, and colorectal cancers is recommended and heavily subsidised for the general population, while lung and liver cancer screening is currently restricted to high-risk individuals due to lower test accuracy and higher false-positive rates in the general population. The Ministry of Health (MOH) advises high-risk individuals to consult their doctors for individualized screening recommendations rather than establishing a separate government subsidy scheme, as allocating specific resources for such a program requires further assessment. MOH continues to promote modifiable risk factors like not smoking and limiting alcohol to address cancer burden. The committee defers to the Screening Test Review Committee's recommendations when determining which populations receive subsidized screening.

results.title: Engaging Camp Vendors or Other Service Providers to Take Care of Children in Affected Little Professors Learning Student Care Centres
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the closure of Little Professors Learning student care centres has already been addressed in the Ministry's response to Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 3 to 6 on 2 March 2026. The transcript does not contain details regarding the specific measures taken, such as the reliance on existing staff, the engagement of camp vendors, or any timeline for alternative arrangements. Consequently, no new policy signal, eligibility rule, or operational constraint was introduced during this exchange.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head K (Ministry of Education)
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) will implement its "Learn For Life, Together" framework to refresh the education system for a fast-changing world, discontinuing the Gifted Education Programme (GEP) in favor of broader access to stretch programmes. While acknowledging that smaller class sizes improve teaching, MOE faces challenges in teacher recruitment and is studying class size calibration; meanwhile, PSLE papers maintain a calibrated balance of 85% easy/moderate and 15% challenging questions to serve as a fair benchmark. MOE will broaden access to school-based programmes for students with strengths in English, mathematics, and science, increase special education school places, and establish new schools for students with moderate to severe special educational needs. Additionally, funding for Mother Tongue Language Learning will increase over the next five years, and employer incentives for the ITE Work-Study Diploma will be enhanced from April 2026. The ministry aims to support disadvantaged students, strengthen holistic development beyond academic grades, and prepare Singaporeans for an AI-transformed future while maintaining academic integrity and data protection.

results.title: Evaluating Platform Delivery Work-Related Risks and Code of Practice for Platform Service to Ensure Riders' Safety
results.summary: From 1 January 2025, the Workplace Safety and Health Act and Work Injury Compensation Act now cover platform work following extensive consultation. The Approved Code of Practice for Platform Services prohibits operators from setting time limits, penalties, or bonuses that encourage risky riding, while workers must not neglect safety for monetary incentives. In September 2025, the Platform Workers Trilateral Group strengthened safeguards by establishing guiding principles ensuring incentives are structured reasonably without compromising health and safety. MOM monitors work-related accidents and will decide on Safety Time-Outs using a calibrated approach that balances heightened vigilance with potential business disruption and worker livelihoods. Courts will consider ACOP compliance during prosecutions.

results.title: Evaluation of Patient Care Outcomes for Community Mental Health Model Across Tiers and Refining Results for Community Services
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the evaluation of patient care outcomes across community mental health tiers, including functional recovery, service utilisation, and hospital escalation, will be addressed at the subsequent Sitting. This response will also cover how these outcomes inform the refinement of Community Outreach Teams (CREST) and Community Intervention Team (COMIT) services. The specific details regarding these evaluations and refinements were not provided in the current transcript.

results.title: Expanding myResponder App’s Scope to Include Alerts for All HDB Residential Fires
results.summary: The Ministry for Home Affairs confirmed there are no plans to expand the myResponder app to alert Community First Responders for all HDB residential fires or introduce a dedicated evacuation tier. This decision stems from the safety risk posed to untrained volunteers when dealing with serious incidents like lithium-ion battery fires, which are reserved for the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF). During HDB fires, SCDF prioritises evacuating residents on the affected floor and those two floors above, while instructing others to remain in safe rooms. For elderly or mobility-impaired residents unable to evacuate independently, authorities advise staying in a secure room, closing doors, and awaiting assistance. SCDF collaborates with grassroots organisations to conduct evacuation drills to prepare for scenarios requiring early evacuation before official arrival.

results.title: Explicit Age-based and Marketing Restrictions in Upcoming Regulations for Blind Boxes
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs directed the Member to refer to the reply to Parliamentary Question No 11, asked by Ms Hazlina Abdul Halim, for the Sitting on 2 March 2026, regarding the regulation of blind boxes. The Minister did not provide new details on proposed age-based restrictions, marketing limits, or impact assessments for minors in this response. The specific measures and timeline for these regulations remain as previously stated in the referenced parliamentary record.

results.title: Extent of Quota Circumvention through Phantom Worker Arrangements
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) investigates phantom worker arrangements via complaints and on-site checks, resulting in enforcement actions against approximately 100 employers between 2024 and 2025 for inflating foreign worker quotas through fraudulent Central Provident Fund contributions. The Minister stated that abolishing Dependency Ratio Ceilings (DRCs) in favour of a flat per-worker tax is not viable, as it would fail to control foreign workforce numbers given lower neighbouring wages and would significantly raise business costs, undermining Singapore's cost-competitiveness. MOM maintains that DRCs ensure firms maintain a local core while deploying foreign workers in roles few locals fill, thereby enhancing workforce resilience. Over the past five years, median gross wages for resident full-time employed PMETs and non-PMETs grew by 3.7% and 5.9% per annum respectively, while resident unemployment has remained below 3% post-COVID-19.

results.title: Feedback from Public and MPs Sought by Committee Tasked to Review Political Office Holders' Salaries
results.summary: The 2026 Committee reviewing political office holders' salaries will not solicit public or MP feedback like the 2011 exercise, as its terms of reference focus on recommending appropriate salary levels within the existing framework reaffirmed in 2017. The Committee will consider information from various sources to propose refinements ensuring the framework remains relevant, but it will not review allowances for legislative and secretarial assistants, which fall outside the current scope. The Committee is still ongoing and will submit its report to the Government for consideration before an update is provided to Parliament.

results.title: Impact of ERP Gantry Removal on Motorist Behaviours and Reducing Congestion
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority is conducting a study to assess how removing physical ERP gantries affects motorists' responsiveness to charges and willingness to divert from priced roads. The study will examine the effectiveness of new visual cues and digital alerts as replacements for physical gantries. Outcomes will inform the design and implementation of ERP 2.0's road guidance features, including road signs, markings, and notifications. Details regarding the study's findings and any potential rate adjustments will be provided in due course.

results.title: Impact of Social Media Use on Students' Learning Outcomes, Attention Span and Social Development
results.summary: A 2024 CNA and Institute of Policy Studies survey indicates Singaporean youths spend an average of 1.5 hours daily on social networking, with anecdotal evidence suggesting higher usage on games and social media. The Ministry of Education acknowledges that excessive use correlates with poorer well-being, reduced attention spans, and negative impacts on learning outcomes and social development, driven by algorithms that extend dwell time and foster echo chambers. To mitigate these risks, MOE has integrated Cyber Wellness lessons into Character and Citizenship Education across primary, secondary, and pre-university levels to teach balance and risk recognition. Additionally, the Ministry of Digital Development and Information, MOE, and the Ministry of Social and Family Development jointly launched the Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media, offering practical tips for youths and parents to build healthy digital relationships. Schools will continue collaborating with parents via platforms like Parents Gateway to share strategies for guiding children's technology use.

results.title: Impact on Inflation with CDC Vouchers Given to Singapore Households Regardless of Socio-economic Status
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated there is no evidence that the Community Development Council (CDC) Vouchers Scheme worsened the cost of living, noting that CPI inflation moderated from 4.8% in 2023 to 0.9% in 2025 despite increased voucher quantum. The Government regularly reviews scheme effectiveness and progressively enhanced the Assurance Package, increasing total support from $6.6 billion in 2022 to over $10 billion in 2025 to offset inflationary expenditure increases for lower- and middle-income households. Additional structural support includes the Workfare Income Supplement Scheme, Fresh Start Housing Scheme, and Silver Support Scheme, complemented by one-off assistance when necessary to remain fiscally prudent. The Minister confirmed that studies on the extent of help provided are conducted through these regular reviews rather than a single specific study.

results.title: Impact on Line Availability from North-East Line Power Supply System Renewal
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed plans to bring forward the North-East Line power supply system renewal by approximately three years, with works commencing in 2026. While the specific impact on service availability remains undetermined at this stage, the Land Transport Authority and the operator commit to minimizing disruptions and providing early notice to commuters of any necessary service adjustments. The original end-of-life schedule has been accelerated, but no further details on the magnitude of the timeline shift or specific operational constraints were provided in the response.

results.title: Increasing Enforcement against Cyclists and E-bike Users for Riding without Helmets, Front and Rear Lights, and Rising against Traffic Flow
results.summary: The Traffic Police and Land Transport Authority conduct regular enforcement against cyclists and e-bike users for offences including riding without helmets, front or rear lights, or against traffic flow, particularly at accident hotspots and locations reported by the public. While the Member raised concerns about motorists facing disproportionate penalties in accidents involving non-compliant riders, authorities stated that culpability is assessed holistically during investigations, ensuring circumstances are fully considered rather than disproportionately blaming one group of road users. Regarding repeat offenders, the Ministry confirmed that cyclists and e-bike users are treated similarly to motorised vehicles, expecting full compliance with traffic rules and applying comparable enforcement measures. The Minister also noted that the approach aligns with the Courts' method of evaluating how accidents are caused.

results.title: Language Distribution of NLB Children's Book Collection and Proportion of Malay Children's Books Published in Bahasa Indonesia
results.summary: The National Library Board (NLB) holds a children's collection of 3.72 million books in English, Chinese, Malay, and Tamil. English titles comprise 74% (approx. 2.75 million), followed by Chinese (18%, approx. 680,300), Malay (5%, approx. 167,100), and Tamil (3%, approx. 118,900). Within the Malay-language segment, approximately 22,500 books (13%) are published in Bahasa Indonesia. These figures represent the current distribution across the NLB borrowing collection for children aged zero to 12 years.

results.title: Limits on Singapore's Total Data Centre Capacity
results.summary: Singapore does not have a pre-determined upper limit on total data centre capacity by 2030, but growth is managed within resource constraints and environmental commitments. Capacity expansion follows a calibrated approach where new allocations are primarily awarded via a competitive Call for Application mechanism. Applicants must demonstrate best-in-class resource efficiency, assessed using internationally recognised metrics such as power and water usage effectiveness. The upcoming Digital Infrastructure Act aims to further uplift energy efficiency and sustainability, with further details to be shared when ready.

results.title: Mandating that Businesses Must Continue to Accept Cash as Payment Option
results.summary: The Minister for Finance directed that the question regarding mandatory cash acceptance, protection for seniors and unbanked youths, and universal access to payment instruments was previously addressed in Minister Chee Hong Tat's reply 1 to Mr Louis Chua's Committee of Supply cut on 26 February 2026. No new measures, timelines, or specific operational constraints were introduced during this session. The parliamentary record indicates that the substantive details of the Ministry's position on cashless-only stores and digital payment requirements remain unchanged from the earlier response.

results.title: Measures to Address Existing Skill Gaps of Singaporean Workers in Government Agencies and Statutory Boards
results.summary: The Government reviews emerging skills needs for public officers, focusing on areas like artificial intelligence, quantum technology, and sustainability. Officers enhance capabilities through formal training, attachments, secondments, and on-the-job learning. The Ministry will continue investing in updating public service capabilities to ensure relevance and future readiness. Specific skill gaps among Singaporeans were not explicitly identified in the response, nor were concrete steps beyond general investment and training mechanisms detailed.

results.title: More Outcome-based Work Arrangements, Rather Than Time-based Models
results.summary: The Ministry has not conducted a specific study correlating flexible work arrangements with productivity, though NTUC survey data ranks such flexibility as the top preferred requirement among workers. While some roles require physical presence, many professionals already operate on outcome-based models, with the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests outlining the process for formal requests based on reasonable business grounds. Employers and workers must mutually agree on outcomes and tracking methods, supported by government initiatives promoting technology adoption and job redesign to enhance efficiency. For public servants and autonomous universities, performance management balances presence requirements with outcome delivery, leaving specific implementation to supervisors and institutions respectively. Resources for flexible work conversations are available through the Tripartite Alliance, Institute for Human Resource Professionals, and NTUC.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head S (Ministry of Manpower)
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower will merge the SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore agencies into a new entity, Workforce and Skills Singapore, to integrate career and skills support under a single portal. While the Government acknowledges diverse views on AI adoption and foreign workforce calibration, it maintains that legislation is not a panacea and that policies must balance worker protection with business flexibility. A Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment will release its report in the second half of 2026, and the qualifying income cap for the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme will be reviewed after gaining further experience. The Government commits to ensuring no worker is left behind, co-funding wage increases to encourage business transformation, and treating fatigue as a core workplace safety issue within its upcoming focus areas.

results.title: Mother Tongue Exemption or Replacement for Students with Conditions Such As Autism Spectrum Disorder or Dyslexia
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) grants Mother Tongue Language exemptions only in exceptional circumstances where special educational needs significantly impact a student's overall learning. Eligibility is determined holistically by assessing performance across all subjects, specific challenges posed by conditions like autism or dyslexia, and the effectiveness of alternative support measures such as the Mother Tongue Support Programme. Schools partner with parents throughout the review process to ensure clarity on criteria and timelines before exemptions are approved. The policy aims to help every student achieve their fullest potential while recognizing unique learning journeys.

results.title: Participation Rate of Tamil Language Elective Programme Teaching Internship in Past Five Years and Subsequent Enrolment at NIE
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, six Tamil Language Elective Programme (TLEP) students participated in the Teaching Internship Programme, with three subsequently enrolling in the National Institute of Education's BA/BSc programme. An additional five TLEP students entered the programme directly, bringing the total to eight students currently completing their degrees. These graduates are expected to become qualified Tamil Language teachers between 2026 and 2029.

results.title: Percentage of HDB BTO Homeowners Reporting Defects that Required Rectification after Key Collection
results.summary: Over the past five years, HDB BTO flats have maintained an average Construction Quality Assessment System score of 95 out of 100, comparable to or exceeding many private developments. New homeowners receive a one-year Defects Liability Period (DLP) to report issues, with an average feedback rate of 65% annually. More than 95% of reported defects are minor, such as scratches or paintwork, and nearly all are rectified within 14 working days, often concurrently with renovation works before move-in. HDB continues to collaborate with construction partners to uphold high workmanship standards and improve the buyer experience.

results.title: Percentage of Households with Utility Bill Arrears
results.summary: Over the past five years, the percentage of households with utility bill arrears of six months or more decreased from 0.5% to 0.2%. The Ministry ensures lower-income households are supported through U-Save rebates for eligible HDB residents and various community assistance schemes. For severe arrears, water supply is not shut off but restricted via flow restrictors, while electricity supply utilizes the Pay-As-You-Use scheme until arrears are settled. The current U-Save rebate framework is tiered by flat type rather than household need. The Ministry noted that expanding rebates to recognize caregiving needs would require further study and gradation of classification, though other existing assistance schemes already address specific caregiving circumstances.

results.title: Placing Road Works Signs at Sufficient Distances to Allow Drivers Time to Safely Change Lanes
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority mandates contractors to place advance warning signs at road worksites according to its Code of Practice for Traffic Control at Work Zone. For instance, on a 50 km/h road, signs must be positioned approximately 280 metres before the worksite, with distances increased for higher speed limits. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that action is taken against contractors for non-compliance with these prescribed requirements. The debate did not address whether the LTA can impose a uniform 200-metre minimum distance across all road types.

results.title: Police Call-outs to Attend to Public Nuisance and Average Time Taken to Resolve Such Disturbances
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force does not track the number of locations with three or more recurring public nuisance callouts nor the average callout frequency before resolution. The Minister clarified that the Police focus on law-and-order concerns, referring nuisance cases without disclosed offences to relevant agencies. For non-arrestable offences, complainants may file Magistrate's Complaints or seek mediation at the Community Mediation Centre. If mediation fails regarding unreasonable interference with a residence, neighbours can file claims with the Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals (CDRT) to obtain orders to stop interference or pay damages. Non-compliance with CDRT orders may lead to Special Directions enforced as criminal offences by the Police. For recurring disturbances, the Police collaborate with stakeholders to assess and address underlying causes rather than responding solely to individual callouts.

results.title: Primary Students on Waitlists for Places in Student Care Centres
results.summary: Approximately 30,000 primary students are enrolled in 182 school-based Student Care Centres (SCCs), which do not operate at the secondary level. While most schools have sufficient capacity, those facing high demand prioritize students from lower-income households lacking adult supervision. A small number of children are on waitlists but currently have alternative care arrangements. For school-based holiday programmes, the Ministry of Education referred to a previous reply given in January 2026. MOE continues collaborating with agencies and community partners to expand capacity and ensure access for students, including those from lower-income families, during both school terms and holidays.

results.title: Projected Domestic Demand for Critical Minerals and Strategies to Ensure Supply Security
results.summary: Singapore's producers primarily import intermediate or final products containing critical minerals for use in semiconductors, AI, medical technology, batteries, and energy storage, rather than raw materials. The government adopts a multi-pronged strategy to ensure supply security by upholding a rules-based international trading system, deepening partnerships with trusted partners, and encouraging firms to diversify sources. Local production capabilities may be developed where economically viable and compliant with environmental and safety regulations, subject to competing national priorities. As an Asian trading hub, Singapore facilitates global flows through trade financing, shipping services, and a deep talent pool, with ongoing efforts to expand trading operations and strengthen the ecosystem for global companies.

results.title: Proportion of HDB Solar Panel Installation Under SolarNova Programme and Future Expansion Plans
results.summary: As of December 2025, approximately 5,300 HDB blocks, representing about half of the total, have solar panels installed under the SolarNova programme and prior efforts. The Housing and Development Board (HDB) will continue installing panels on remaining blocks subject to site suitability. The Minister confirmed this proportion will be increased in the future through continued expansion of the programme. No specific timeline or target percentage for future installation was provided in the response.

results.title: Relationship-based Support to Help Seniors Rebuild Daily Routines and Social Connections
results.summary: The Government offers structured relationship-based support under Age Well SG to help seniors rebuild routines and social connections. Silver Generation Ambassadors connect isolated seniors to buddying services at Active Ageing Centres (AACs), where community befrienders visit monthly. AACs facilitate engagement through communal dining and volunteering. Seniors with complex conditions like dementia or depression receive targeted referrals to Dementia Day Care centres for cognitive stimulation or to Community Outreach Teams (CREST) for mental health screening and emotional support. These measures ensure meaningful engagement across general and specialized care pathways.

results.title: Review Vetting Process of Student and Kindergarten Care Service Providers and Enhance Financial Safeguards and Monitoring Mechanisms
results.summary: The Ministry of Education states that the review of vetting processes for student and kindergarten care service providers, including financial safeguards and monitoring mechanisms, has already been addressed in its response to Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 3 to 6 on 2 March 2026. No new measures, timelines, or additional operational constraints were announced during this session. The Minister indicated that the existing answer covers the inquiry regarding preventing recurrence of operational disruptions caused by vendor weaknesses.

results.title: Review of SG Heritage Business Scheme Eligibility Criteria and Total Amount of Funds Disbursed
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth clarified that the SG Heritage Business Scheme, administered by the National Heritage Board (NHB), does not provide direct funding but offers branding, marketing guidance, and tailored consultancy starting March 2026. Eligibility requires businesses to maintain heritage status in the same location for at least 30 years, demonstrate strong community impact, and offer traditional trades or services. While the initial phase launched in the Central Area in March 2025 with 42 designated businesses, NHB is reviewing parameters to expand recognition beyond this zone. Heritage businesses may alternatively access the Organisation Transformation Grant, awarded to 56 entities as of Q4 2025, or other grants like the Productivity Solutions Grant to enhance sustainability and productivity.

results.title: Reviewing CDC Vouchers Eligibility in View of Distinct Family Nuclei Residing in Same Address for Caregiving Purposes
results.summary: The Ministry will consider reviewing CDC Vouchers eligibility to accommodate distinct family nuclei residing at the same address for caregiving purposes, such as married children living with parents. While the CDC Vouchers Scheme targets all households, it is complemented by other schemes like the Silver Support Scheme and Home Caregiving Grant to support varying family circumstances. The Government noted that unrelated households in the same address can currently appeal for separate status, but related households in multi-generational setups faced difficulties as appeals were unsuccessful. The Ministry acknowledged feedback regarding households with eight to nine members unable to access vouchers and will take the suggestion to calibrate support based on household size back for consideration.

results.title: Risk Assessments of Bus Arrival Timing System Prior to January 2026 System Glitch
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority conducted a risk assessment on the Expected Time of Arrival (ETA) system in July 2024, as required biennially, identifying minor issues resolved before the January 2026 disruption. The assessment failed to detect the specific vulnerabilities that caused the recent system glitch. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that a review of the risk assessment methodology will be undertaken to improve future iterations. Independent system audits and stress test frequencies were not specified in the response.

results.title: Segregated Account Requirements for Businesses that Collect Client Monies through Prepayment Packages
results.summary: The Government will not immediately mandate segregated accounts for businesses collecting prepayment monies but is reviewing existing protections under the Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act 2003. An independent Consumer Protection Review Panel was convened in March 2025 to assess concerns regarding high-value packages, including the adequacy of current safeguards. The Panel is expected to share findings and recommendations later this year. Meanwhile, consumers of CaseTrust-accredited beauty businesses can claim refunds for unused prepayments if the business closes, supported by mandatory prepayment protection insurance.

results.title: Reforming CCA Access in Our Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education addresses concerns that the current Co-Curricular Activities (CCA) system, reliant on selection trials and early specialization, limits access for students unable to afford private coaching. Officials characterize the current landscape as a "system problem" involving space constraints and grading considerations rather than a lack of student ability, noting that early selection at age 12 is misaligned with evidence favoring multi-disciplinary engagement for world-class performance. To widen access, the government proposes three specific reforms: opening developmental tiers within schools, enabling cross-school access beyond current boundaries, and building infrastructure through lighter coaching credentials and revised LEAPS incentives. Additionally, students from lower-income families can utilize the MOE Opportunity Fund for CCA expenses, while NROC certification remains mandatory for coaches to ensure safety. The Ministry confirms it will continue reviewing the system to ensure CCAs remain an integral part of holistic education rather than mere extras.

results.title: Special Educational Needs Examination Access Arrangement Timelines and Study of Information-sharing Streamlining
results.summary: The Ministry of Education and Singapore Examinations and Assessment Board (SEAB) allow examination access arrangement applications one year before, and no later than two months before, the exam date. Since January 2025, students with previously approved arrangements for the same medical condition no longer need to resubmit diagnostic reports for subsequent national examinations. Only complex requests requiring specialised support, such as readers and scribes, continue to require updated medical reports. The Ministry is committed to streamlining information-sharing while maintaining appropriate consent safeguards to prevent parents from repeatedly submitting similar assessments across schooling stages.

results.title: Standard Protocols for ComLink+ Family Coaches to Contact Disengaged Households
results.summary: Approximately 10% of ComLink+ families remain unresponsive despite multiple contact attempts via calls, texts, or home visits. Social Service Offices collaborate with community partners, including schools and grassroots organisations, to facilitate engagement. Where abuse or domestic violence concerns exist, Family Service Centres may invoke powers under the Children and Young Persons Act or Women's Charter to compel access to children. The Ministry of Social and Family Development's Protective Service can also be engaged to assess cases and determine appropriate interventions for persistently uncontactable households. Specific statistics on families remaining uncontactable after three, six, nine, or 12 months as of December 2025 were not provided in the response.

results.title: Strengthening Long-term Healthcare Workforce Sustainability while Maintaining Care Standards
results.summary: The Ministry prioritizes building a local core workforce through diversified training, competitive salaries, and bilateral agreements to secure foreign manpower in shortfall areas. To ensure sustainability, care models are shifting toward shared care and team-based approaches, while new career tracks like "hospital clinicians" and a designated family medicine specialty aim to retain experienced professionals and align with community care transformation. Retention strategies include seamless career progression, inclusive work environments led by Chief Welfare Officers, and a zero-tolerance stance on harassment. Regarding foreign domestic workers, the Ministry explicitly stated there are no plans to professionalize them into formal healthcare roles or allow their transition into the community care sector as nurses or care staff.

results.title: Timeline of Culture Pass Platform Onboarding Process and Price Review Mechanism for Events
results.summary: Since December 2025, MCCY processes complete Culture Pass applications within one month, while incomplete ones trigger applicant engagement. Safeguards ensure pricing consistency with non-Culture Pass offerings; as of January 2026, average ticket prices are approximately $50, remaining largely unchanged from pre-introduction levels. These measures prevent inflated pricing and allow Singaporeans to use credits for at least two programmes. The Minister confirmed no specific price review mechanism exists beyond these established safeguards.

results.title: Tracking User Satisfaction with ComLink+, Including Experience with Family Coaches
results.summary: The Minister stated that MSF does not formally collect feedback from ComLink+ families regarding their experience with family coaches, though families may provide specific feedback directly to their Social Service Officers. New coaches undergo structured foundational training in family assessment, engagement techniques, and cultural competency, supported by a Practice Guide to codify good practices. The programme aims to help lower-income households achieve outcomes in Stability, Self-reliance, and Social Mobility across six domains, including income security, children's development, housing, family functioning, financial resilience, and health. Service quality is maintained through these training standards and the firm yet empathetic approach adopted by coaches to ensure sustainable outcomes.

results.title: Tracking of Singaporean Banks' Revenue from Payment Networks and Evaluation of Contractual Risks in Past Four Years
results.summary: Card revenue, including interchange, network, and co-branding fees, averaged approximately 3% of Singapore banks' total revenue over the past four years, as disclosed in annual financial statements. The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) does not separately track these specific fee components but encourages competition to foster innovation in payment services. MAS promotes a diverse range of digital payment solutions, noting that domestic schemes like NETS EFTPOS, PayNow, and Singapore QR Code offer significantly lower costs compared to international card schemes. The authority's approach focuses on maintaining a competitive landscape rather than imposing specific tracking mandates on these revenue streams.

results.title: Use of Foreign Worker Levy to Improve Transport Safety for Migrant Workers over Past Three Years
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that tax revenues, including foreign worker levies, are not ringfenced for specific spending, so no separate expenditure amount was directed to safer transport for migrant workers in the past three years. Consequently, the Ministry will not allocate dedicated levy funds for this purpose but will continue collaborating with industry and non-governmental organisations to improve worker transport safety. The Government acknowledged known risks in current practices but maintains that general consolidated revenue supports these safety initiatives rather than specific levy allocations.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of Edusave and PSEA Savings Account in Past 10 Years
results.summary: As of February 2026, aggregate balances in Edusave and Post-Secondary Education Accounts (PSEA) stand at $0.5 billion and $2.1 billion respectively. Over the past five years, the average Edusave utilisation rate prior to transfer was 50%, with an average transfer amount of $1,355 per member; approximately 2% of members had zero balance at transfer. PSEA accounts close at age 31, transferring remaining balances to the CPF-Ordinary Account, where 59% of balances remained unspent in the last five years. The Minister noted data availability covers only the past five years rather than the full ten-year period requested.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of SG60 Vouchers by Age Group and Additional Measures to Encourage Greater Utilisation
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that all Singapore Citizens aged 21 and above received $600 in SG60 vouchers in 2025, with seniors aged 60 and above receiving an additional $200 for a total of $800. Between 2021 and 2025, $3.995 billion in Community Development Council and SG60 vouchers were spent, achieving an average claim rate of 97.7% across seven tranches. Of this total, $2.127 billion supported heartland shops and hawkers, while $1.868 billion was spent at supermarkets. The vouchers are redeemable at 24,000 participating heartland merchants, hawkers, and over 400 supermarket outlets. Beyond grassroots outreach, the People's Association engages partners like merchants' and hawkers' associations to encourage businesses to assist customers in claiming and spending their vouchers.
